title: HDB Resale Price — Model Training Journey

sections.heading: 1. Timeline
sections.snippet: v1 $25,943 → v9 $21,756 → v16 OOF $21,552 → Team Ens-1 $21,350.91 → v20 Kaggle $21,827 (overfit) → 3member-equal-v20 $21,345.60 → 3member-wt-v20 $21,335.57 NEW BEST. Gap to 1st ~$93.

sections.heading: 2. Quick Reference Table
sections.snippet: v1–v20. Team ensemble Apr 29: equal-weight v20 $21,345.60, weighted v20 $21,335.57 NEW BEST (–$212 vs best individual). Weighted gives Lanson ~36% — highest weight pays off. Gap to 1st ~$93.

sections.heading: 3. CV vs Kaggle Gap Trend
sections.snippet: Gap trend: v5 $858 (target leakage), v6 $306 (OOF fix), v7 $65 (stacking), v8 $97, v9 ~$47. Team ensemble gap not applicable — cross-member blend. Best Kaggle $21,350.91 (Team Ensemble). Gap to 1st ~$108.

sections.heading: 4. Version Detail Cards
sections.snippet: v20 all Optuna: OOF $21,465, Kaggle $21,827 (overfit gap $362). Team Ens-1 Apr 28: weighted $21,350.91. Team Ens-2 Apr 29: 3member-equal-v20 $21,345.60 NEW BEST. Key insight: v20 errors partially uncorrelated with Lanson/Likhong — ensemble cancels them even though individual score was worse.

sections.heading: 5. Cumulative Feature Engineering
sections.snippet: FeatureAdded inWhat it capturesIn model? remaining_leasev299 – (year – lease_commence). More interpretable than age.yes dist_to_cbdv2Haversine km to Raffles Place. #2 feature importance in XGB.yes is_

sections.heading: 6. What's Next
sections.snippet: Best: $21,335.57 (3member-wt-v20). Gap to 1st ~$93. Next: 3member-rank-v20 still untried. Optimise blend weights via OOF. Retune CatBoost with 5-fold CV to fix overfit gap.

sections.heading: 1. What Was Wrong With v7?
sections.snippet: v7 used two base models — tuned XGBoost and tuned LightGBM — with a Ridge meta-model trained on their out-of-fold (OOF) predictions. It scored $21,906 on Kaggle. Both XGB and LGBM are gradient boostin

sections.heading: 2. What Is Extra Trees (Extra Randomized Trees)?
sections.snippet: Extra Trees (ExtraTreesRegressor) is a bagging ensemble of decision trees — but with one extra twist compared to Random Forest: the split thresholds are chosen completely randomly. Property Random For

sections.heading: 3. The v8 Architecture
sections.snippet: Training data (5-fold CV) │ ├── → XGBoost (tuned) → xgb_oof[n_train] ├── → LightGBM (tuned) → lgbm_oof[n_train] └── → Extra Trees (tuned) → et_oof[n_train] │ ▼ Ridge meta-model trained on [xgb_oof, lg

sections.heading: 4. Out-of-Fold (OOF) Predictions — Why and How
sections.snippet: OOF is the key that makes stacking honest. Here's the 5-fold process for one base model (same for all three): for fold 1 of 5: train XGB on folds 2,3,4,5 → predict on fold 1 → xgb_oof[fold_1_rows] for

sections.heading: 5. Individual Base Model Performance
sections.snippet: Model Validation RMSE (5-fold avg) Role in stack XGBoost (tuned) $21,769 Primary boosting signal — highest individual accuracy LightGBM (tuned) $21,808 Leaf-wise boosting — correlated with XGB but sub

sections.heading: 6. Ridge Meta-Model — How It Weights the Three Models
sections.snippet: Ridge regression (linear regression with L2 regularisation) learns coefficients for each base model's OOF predictions. The coefficients tell us how much the meta-model trusts each base model: XGBoost

sections.heading: 7. Hyperparameter Tuning — What Changed vs v7
sections.snippet: XGB and LGBM used the same tuned hyperparameters as v7. We ran RandomizedSearchCV (20 iterations, 5-fold CV) specifically for Extra Trees: et_param_dist = { 'n_estimators': [300, 500, 700, 1000], 'max

sections.heading: 8. Complete v8 Pipeline Code
sections.snippet: from sklearn.ensemble import ExtraTreesRegressor from sklearn.linear_model import Ridge from sklearn.model_selection import KFold import numpy as np kf = KFold(n_splits=5, shuffle=True, random_state=4

sections.heading: 9. Why the Stack OOF RMSE ($21,708) Is Lower Than Kaggle ($21,804)
sections.snippet: This is a healthy, expected gap. The OOF RMSE is measured on training data — rows the base models weren't trained on within that fold, but still rows from the same distribution as the full training se

sections.heading: 10. Summary: What Made v8 Better
sections.snippet: Change from v7 → v8 Effect Added ExtraTreesRegressor as 3rd base model More diverse OOF signal for meta-model Ridge now fits on 3 OOF columns instead of 2 Better weighted blend — ET adds uncorrelated

sections.heading: Glossary
sections.snippet: TermPlain-English meaning OOF (Out-of-Fold)Predictions made on the held-out validation fold — never seen during that model's training. Honest predictions. Base modelFirst-level model that predicts the

sections.heading: Why We Built This
sections.snippet: 17+ experiment notebooks each contain ~300-400 lines of duplicated boilerplate: data loading, feature engineering, OOF encoding loop, 5-fold stacking, Ridge blending, and submission generation. To test a new hyperparameter or add an OOF feature, you had to copy the entire notebook and edit cells inline. With the automated pipeline, every new experiment edits only one CONFIG dict. MLflow auto-logs every run for easy comparison.

sections.heading: Architecture — What Lives Where
sections.snippet: src/features/feature_engineering.py — engineer_features() and all constants. src/features/oof_encoding.py — apply_global_oof_encodings(). src/models/stacker.py — run_stacking() 5-fold OOF loop for 4 base models. src/models/meta.py — ridge_blend() alpha sweep and meta weights. src/pipeline.py — run_pipeline(CONFIG) orchestrates everything. notebooks/experiments/template_experiment.ipynb — only CONFIG cell + run_pipeline() call.

sections.heading: The CONFIG Dict — Every Key Explained
sections.snippet: experiment_name: MLflow run label and experiment name. data.train_path / test_path: CSV paths. features.encode_pairs: list of (group_col, feature_name, min_count) tuples for OOF target encodings. features.drop_cols: extra columns to exclude from training matrix. models.active: list of base models to include (xgb, lgbm, et, catboost). models.ridge_alphas: alpha sweep for Ridge meta-learner. cv.n_splits: number of folds. output.submission_path: OOF submission CSV. output.fulldata_submission_path: optional 100% retrain submission.

sections.heading: Running a New Experiment — Step by Step
sections.snippet: 1. Copy template_experiment.ipynb and rename it (e.g. 19_my_experiment.ipynb). 2. Edit Cell 1 only: change experiment_name, adjust hyperparams, encode_pairs, drop_cols. 3. Run All Cells in the ml conda environment. 4. Cell 3 prints OOF RMSE, Ridge weights, best alpha. 5. Compare runs: mlflow ui --backend-store-uri sqlite:///mlflow.db then open http://127.0.0.1:5000.

sections.heading: Changing Hyperparameters
sections.snippet: Edit the relevant model dict inside CONFIG. Key XGBoost params: n_estimators (trees), max_depth (tree depth), learning_rate (step size), min_child_weight (minimum leaf weight), colsample_bytree (feature fraction). Key LightGBM params: num_leaves (leaf count, default 31), min_child_samples (minimum leaf samples). Key CatBoost params: iterations, depth, learning_rate.

sections.heading: Adding a New OOF Target Encoding Feature
sections.snippet: Add a tuple to encode_pairs: (group_column, new_feature_name, min_group_count). The group_column must exist in the raw data or be created by engineer_features(). min_group_count filters rare groups — set to 1 for all groups, higher to reduce noise. The encoding computes per-fold median resale price for each group value, preventing target leakage.

sections.heading: Two Submission Modes — OOF vs 100% Retrain
sections.snippet: OOF mode (default): each base model trains on 80% of data per fold, test predictions are averaged over 5 folds. This is honest — each model never saw the rows it predicts. 100% retrain mode: uncomment fulldata_submission_path in output. Ridge weights stay fixed from OOF step; only base models retrain on all 150K rows. Typically improves Kaggle RMSE by $50-100. Takes ~30 minutes extra.

sections.heading: MLflow — Tracking All Experiments
sections.snippet: MLflow automatically logs all hyperparameters, OOF RMSE per model, Ridge blend weights, and best alpha for every run. Start the UI: cd to project root, then run: mlflow ui --backend-store-uri sqlite:///mlflow.db. Open http://127.0.0.1:5000. Compare experiments by OOF RMSE column, filter by experiment name, view Ridge weights to see which model each run relied on most.

sections.heading: Verification — How We Know It Works
sections.snippet: Pipeline test notebook 18_v16_pipeline_test.ipynb reproduced v16 metrics to 4 decimal places: OOF RMSE $21,552, XGB weight 0.3853, LGBM weight 0.4824, ET weight 0.1368, CatBoost weight 0.0159, best alpha 0.001. All fold scores match within rounding. MLflow run confirmed logged with correct params and metrics.

sections.heading: Quick Reference
sections.snippet: Change hyperparams: edit models.xgb / models.lgbm / etc. Add OOF feature: add tuple to features.encode_pairs. Remove OOF feature: delete tuple from features.encode_pairs. Exclude column from training: add to features.drop_cols. Exclude a model: remove from models.active. Enable 100% retrain: uncomment output.fulldata_submission_path. View all runs: mlflow ui --backend-store-uri sqlite:///mlflow.db.

sections.heading: Project Overview
sections.snippet: 150,634 HDB resale transactions. Target: resale_price (SGD). Best Kaggle RMSE ,756 (v9). Total improvement from v1 baseline: -,187 (-16.1%). Gap to 1st place: .

sections.heading: Data Pipeline Steps
sections.snippet: End-to-end pipeline: raw data ingestion, drop redundant columns, fill missing values, feature engineering (16 features), OOF target encoding, log-transform target, 5-fold KFold, RandomizedSearchCV tuning, OOF prediction generation, Ridge meta-learner, test prediction averaging.

sections.heading: Feature Importance Analysis
sections.snippet: Top features by model gain: floor_area_sqm (dominant PC1), town_median_price OOF, postal_sector_median_price OOF, mid_storey, remaining_lease, dist_to_cbd, flat_model_median_price OOF, amenity_score, is_mature_estate. Engineered features help XGBoost more than RF.

sections.heading: Training Methods and Models
sections.snippet: v1 RF baseline. v3 XGBoost tuned (-,781). v5 blend. v6 log target + OOF encoding. v7 Ridge stack XGB+LGBM. v8 3-model stack +ExtraTrees. v9 richer encoding + wider grid. Final: Ridge equation 0.3854xXGB + 0.4824xLGBM + 0.1347xET - 0.0318.

sections.heading: Validation Strategy
sections.snippet: 5-fold KFold shuffle random_state=42. OOF predictions prevent meta-model leakage. OOF target encoding prevents feature leakage (town, postal_sector, flat_model). Double OOF inside stacking loop. CV-Kaggle gap diagnostic: v5 gap  (leakage) collapsed to v7 gap  (excellent).

sections.heading: Results Progression
sections.snippet: v1 ,943 to v3 ,801 (-,781 switch to XGB) to v5 ,428 (blend) to v6 ,124 (leakage fix) to v7 ,906 (stacking) to v8 ,805 (ExtraTrees) to v9 ,756 (richer encoding). Total -,187.

sections.heading: Key Learnings
sections.snippet: Algorithm choice beats feature engineering. OOF encoding fixes leakage measurably. OOF stacking collapses CV-Kaggle gap. Model diversity (bagging + boosting) beats homogeneous ensembles. Geographic granularity (postal sector > town) is high-value in Singapore housing market.
